Where nature leads the kitchen

01 — Pillar
Innovation Through Tailor Thinking

No templates. Every concept built from scratch — from the place, the producer, and the people it will serve. Innovation means making something that could only exist here, now.

03 — Pillar
Research & Development

We develop ideas, menus, flavours, experiences, ingredients, and beverages — with deep foundations in fermentation science, fire techniques, and a restless curiosity about what food can be.

02 — Pillar
Sustainability as a Way of Life

Not a program. Not a section. A philosophy embedded in every sourcing decision, every system we design, every relationship we build with the land and the people who work it.

04 — Pillar
The Human Touch

Done by humans, for humans — with the support of technology but never replaced by it. Every concept we build carries the mark of hands, decisions, and care no algorithm can replicate.

Based in Phuket, Thailand
The studio

Where ideas ferment and restaurants come to life

Cultivate Studio is an R&D-led culinary studio founded by Executive Chef Marco Turatti — Italian-born, 16+ years building restaurants and bars across Europe and Southeast Asia. We don't just consult. We research, develop, and build.

Our work starts from the ground: the farmer, the fisher, the fermentation jar, the fire. We build the sourcing infrastructure first, then move backwards into the kitchen. The concept, the menu, the team — all of it grows from that foundation.

Every project we take on gets the same approach: deep research into place and ingredient, a concept built from scratch with no borrowed templates, and an operational system designed to sustain what we build long after we leave.

"The menu is the last step. The farm, the fisher, the fermentation jar — that is where the work begins."

The studio

How we build a restaurant

This is where the thinking happens. The concept creation process is a sequence of layered decisions — each one building on the last. Every engagement begins here, in the studio, before a single dish is cooked or a single menu is written.

Research is not academic here. It is operational.

"Every concept begins with an ingredient no one else is paying attention to."

// Concept creation process
01
Discovery
Place, people, market, culture. What does this location want to be?
02
Ingredient Research
Sourcing network, producers, rare ingredients, seasonal rhythms.
03
Concept Architecture
Identity, format, name, culinary philosophy. The concept pillars.
04
R&D & Menu Build
Fermentation, fire, flavour development. Recipes tested and documented.
05
Menu & Food Training
Team trained on every dish, technique, and ingredient story. The concept moves from paper to hands.
06
Team & Operations
Kitchen structure, staffing model, salary benchmarking. Building the team that sustains the concept.
07
Marketing Positioning
Media strategy, content pillars, photography direction, press angle. How the world first hears about this restaurant.
08
Traditional Marketing
PR, guide outreach, influencer strategy, opening event. Building visibility before and after the first service.
09
Launch & Evolve
Opening support, first-year review, seasonal menu evolution. The concept grows from here.
Built from the inside out

Cultivate IntelligenceChef & F&B Platform

The tools we use every day to go deeper, think sharper, and open more doors. Not software that does the work — but intelligence that makes the work better. Flavour architecture, sourcing data, concept mapping, sustainability tracking — built to push our thinking further, surface connections we might otherwise miss, and create the conditions for better ideas and bolder experiments.

01
Chef Intelligence & Flavour Matrix
12-axis flavour scoring · pairing logic · tension mapping · menu analysis
02
Recipe Manager & Cost Engine
Multi-outlet · nutrition · waste · food cost · XLSX export
03
Concept & Strategy Builder
20-day sprint · 90-day plan · 12-month roadmap with SE Asia benchmarks
04
Sustainability & Producer Network
FMG scorecard · waste tracker · Green Star framework · supplier directory
05
Cultivate AI
Fine dining & F&B intelligence · technique · trends · costing · seasonality

Active research

The studio at work

Step four of the process — and the most alive. This is where concepts become food. Fermentation programs running in parallel with menu development. Fire techniques tested against flavour hypotheses. Recipes documented, challenged, refined. The R&D phase is where the concept stops being an idea and starts being something you can taste.

R&D · Fermentation & Preservation
Building flavour from the ground up

Across InterContinental Phuket Resort, The Studio runs an active fermentation program producing tomato vinegar, jackfruit vinegar, mangostein vinegar, plum vinegar, and dozens of other live cultures — all from locally sourced Thai ingredients. These are not garnishes. They are the flavour architecture of the menus, replacing imported acids and umami sources with something alive and specific to this place.

Active program · IC Phuket Resort
R&D · Koji & Miso Development
Koji, miso & amino pastes

Carrot mustard miso, stinky bean miso, sunflower peaso — The Studio's koji and miso program applies Japanese fermentation technique to Southern Thai ingredients. Every paste is labelled, dated, and tracked through its full fermentation arc.

Active · IC Phuket fermentation lab
R&D · Live Culture Research
Inside the fermentation jar

Buckwheat shrimp tamari, lacto-fermented vegetables, garum in development. Each jar is a live experiment — documented, observed, tasted weekly. The results inform both the menu and future projects.

Active · ongoing documentation
R&D · Koji Culture
Growing koji on local grains

Aspergillus oryzae cultivated on Thai jasmine rice, barley, and corn. Each batch produces the enzymes that unlock umami across the menu — a living culture replacing imported ingredients entirely.

Live culture · Phuket lab
R&D · Ingredient Extraction
Infusions & extractions

Wild herb infusions, botanical extractions, and preserved aromatics. Southern Thai botanicals captured at peak and held — for sauces, dressings, and beverage applications across multiple outlets.

Active · seasonal program
R&D → Plate · IC Phuket
From lab to service

Wood sorrel, micro herbs, red weaver ants. The research becomes the dish. Every element on this plate went through the R&D process — sourced, tested, fermented, refined, then placed with intention.

Live menu · Michelin-listed hom & Jaras
The pantry

Ingredients worth knowing

These are not exotic imports. They grow in the soil, swim in the water, and ferment in the jars of Southern Thailand. Understanding them deeply — their provenance, their seasonality, their transformation — is where every concept we build begins.

Southern Thailand · Zingiberaceae
Torch Ginger Flower

Etlingera elatior — the bud is layered, floral, and faintly sour. Unlike any other aromatic in Thai cooking. Used raw in kerabu, slow-braised in curries, and as a fermentation substrate at hom. Impossible to replicate with any imported ingredient.

Active · hom & Jaras
Gulf of Thailand · Fermentation
Kung Dried Shrimp Tamari

Small dried shrimp from the Gulf of Thailand fermented with buckwheat koji into a tamari that carries the sea in every drop. An amino sauce that replaces imported soy — built entirely from the Thai coastline.

Fermentation lab · hom
Southern Thailand · Wild harvest
Pak Wan Pa

Wild-harvested forest greens from the hills behind Phuket. Mildly bitter, deeply aromatic. Almost unknown outside the region. We infuse, pickle, and ferment them — capturing the flavor of a specific hillside at a specific moment.

Wild harvest · seasonal
Southern Thailand · Fermented
Sator / Stinky Bean Miso

Parkia speciosa — the parkia bean with its sulfurous, deeply savory character fermented with koji into a miso that tastes like nowhere else on earth. A Southern Thai ingredient transformed by Japanese technique.

Fermentation lab · hom
Fermentation Lab · Phuket
Thai Jasmine Rice Koji

Aspergillus oryzae grown on Surin Basin jasmine rice — one of Thailand's most aromatic varieties. The koji's enzymes are calibrated to this specific grain's starch structure. The result is a culture that smells and tastes distinctly Thai, not Japanese.

Live culture · ongoing
Southern Thailand · Foraged
Mod Daeng — Red Weaver Ant

Harvested from mango trees in the orchards of Southern Thailand. Intensely citric, alive, and slightly acidic. Used at hom as a finishing element that delivers a flavor no vinegar can replicate — the taste of the tree, the season, and the hand that picked them.

Foraged · Michelin-listed hom
Sustainability

Built in. Not bolted on.

Four operational systems running inside a live, nine-outlet hotel kitchen — tested daily against volume, quality, and cost. Not pledges. Infrastructure. And the expertise to replicate every one of them in your next project.

We bring this knowledge
to every new project.

The sustainability systems built at InterContinental Phuket are not site-specific solutions. They are replicable frameworks — designed, tested, and documented to be implemented in any luxury hotel or restaurant operation. Every consulting engagement we take on includes sustainability program design as a core deliverable, not an afterthought.

We work with international certification platforms to formalise what we build — including Food Made Good, the global sustainability framework for the restaurant industry, currently active in our Phuket operation.

Food Made Good · Active WWF Partnership Zero single-use plastic
90%
Thailand sourced
24h
Compost cycle
Zero
Single-use plastic
90%
Thailand

90% Thailand sourced.
South Thailand first, always.

Across all nine outlets at InterContinental Phuket, 90% of all ingredients are sourced within Thailand — with a deliberate bias toward Southern Thailand producers. The remaining 10% covers items where local production cannot meet the required standard. A procurement discipline built into every order cycle — not a marketing figure.

Thailand sourced (90%)
Selective import (10%)
// 01 — Invasive Species
WWF Invasive Species Program · Jaras

In partnership with WWF, we run an active invasive species culinary program at Jaras. Species threatening local marine and ecological biodiversity are responsibly harvested and integrated into the menu — transforming an environmental problem into a sustainable food source and a compelling guest story.

Research covers preparation, flavour profiling, nutritional analysis, and consumer acceptance. Each species has a documented protocol reviewed with WWF partners. This program is available as a consulting deliverable for coastal properties.

Active · WWF partnership
// 02 — Zero Waste
Oklin Composting System

Organic kitchen waste from all nine outlets processed into finished compost within 24 hours. Output feeds the resort farm and is distributed to the surrounding community — closing the loop between kitchen waste and the soil that grows our produce.

Accelerated aerobic decomposition eliminates methane emissions. Output tracked monthly. Community distribution extends impact beyond the property. Full system design and implementation available as a consulting service.

Zero waste · Farm & community
// 03 — Zero Plastic
Full-Property Water Filtration

Full-property system eliminating single-use plastic water bottles across all hotel restaurants, bars, and guest services. Filtered still and sparkling water served across all nine outlets — plastic removed from the guest experience entirely.

Infrastructure planning, supplier coordination, and team training across all departments. Applicable to any hotel F&B operation — design and implementation available as a consulting deliverable.

Zero single-use plastic
// 04 — Producer Network
Resort Farm & Southern Thailand Network

Working farm on the resort property supplying the kitchen daily. Direct producer network across Southern Thailand — farmers, fishers, and artisan producers integrated into menus across all outlets and seasons.

Building a direct producer network is a core consulting deliverable — we apply the same methodology we used in Southern Thailand to any region where a new property is opening.

Live farm · Active network
Consulting

Building operations worth sustaining

A limited number of engagements per year, taken selectively. We work with luxury hotels, resort developers, and F&B groups who want something that lasts beyond the opening.

01
Concept Architecture

From zero to first service. Culinary identity, menu logic, kitchen systems, and sourcing frameworks built for luxury hotels and independent restaurants. Every concept starts from place, producer, and purpose.

New openingsLuxury hotelsIdentity
02
F&B Strategy

Multi-outlet direction covering revenue structure, menu engineering, team hierarchy, and supplier partnerships. Currently active across IHG brands in Southeast Asia. Built and executed using our proprietary Cultivate Intelligence platform — Strategy Builder, P&L analysis, and benchmarking tools.

Multi-outletIHG / chainsRevenue
03
Sustainable Operations

Designing and implementing sustainability infrastructure — composting, water filtration, local sourcing protocols, Food Made Good certification, and zero-waste programs — that function at full hotel scale.

Zero wasteFood Made GoodInfrastructure
04
Menu Development & R&D

Research-driven menus integrating seasonal rhythms, local producers, and fermentation programs. From Michelin-level tasting formats to elevated casual — the same R&D rigour applied to both. Every menu is built through our Chef Intelligence & Flavour Matrix — a 12-axis flavour architecture system that maps pairing logic, tension pairs, and seasonal ingredient intelligence.

Fine diningCasualFermentation
05
Social Media & Brand Positioning

Defining the visual and editorial identity of a restaurant concept for social media — content pillars, photography direction, story angles, and platform strategy. Helping concepts build audiences before and after opening.

InstagramContent strategyBrand voice
06
Pre-Opening Structure & Budget

Pro-forma budget creation for pre-opening and year one. Kitchen manning structure, salary benchmarking, and talent connection — helping new concepts find the right people without the cost of a recruiter. We get the word out.

Pro-formaKitchen structureTalent network

// Open to new projects

Building something worth sustaining?

We take a limited number of projects per year. If you're opening a luxury property or looking for a research-led culinary partner, let's talk.

Start the conversation
InterContinental Phuket Resort

Nine outlets. One culinary vision.

Leading the full culinary operation at InterContinental Phuket Resort — managing teams, menus, and sourcing programs across nine distinct venues. From two Michelin-listed restaurants and a World's 50 Best Discovery to fire-inspired casual, Peruvian beach dining, and a botanical cocktail bar. One island. One kitchen philosophy. Nine expressions.

Thai Contemporary Fine Dining
Jaras

The flagship. Southern Thai cuisine built on 90% local sourcing — a farmer network across the peninsula, a resort farm, and relationships with fishermen who've supplied the kitchen for years. Jaras is where the land and the sea of Southern Thailand become a tasting menu. Awarded, recognised, and still evolving.

Michelin Guide Thailand Thailand's Favourite Restaurant WWF Seafood Partnership Sustainability Award
Modern Thai Fine Dining
hom

Modern Thai cuisine at its most considered. A tasting menu format with the discipline of fine dining and the soul of the Thai landscape. One of the most recognised tables in the country.

Michelin Guide Thailand World's 50 Best Discovery T+L Tastemaker Award Tatler Asia's 100 Best Tatler Thailand Best
Japanese Fine Casual
Tengoku

Precision Japanese cooking in a fine casual format. Omakase sensibility applied to an accessible experience — local Andaman seafood interpreted through Japanese technique. Ranked among the top restaurants on the island.

Top 25 Restaurants Phuket
Breakfast · Fire-Inspired Dinner
Pinto

Where the day starts and fire ends it. Breakfast built on local produce and the resort farm. Dinner driven by live fire — charcoal grill, tandoor, fire pit, and pizza oven. Every cook exposed to the fundamentals of live-fire technique.

Charcoal grill · Tandoor Fire pit · Pizza oven Resort farm produce
Modern World Cuisine · Premium Casual
Pine Beach Bar & Pine Rooftop

Two venues, one ocean view. Pine Beach Bar anchors the beachfront with modern world cuisine in a premium casual setting — lighter, social, all-day. Pine Rooftop takes it vertical: sunset drinks, elevated bites, and the Andaman at golden hour. Both spaces serve the same culinary philosophy — technique-led, locally sourced, globally inspired.

Peruvian-Inspired · Local Sourcing
333 at the Beach

Peru's coastal culinary tradition — ceviche, tiradito, causas, anticuchos — reframed through Andaman ingredients. Fresh-caught local fish, tropical acidity, and the fire of ají amarillo. Relaxed in format. Precise in execution.

Andaman seafood sourcing Peruvian technique · local application
Patisserie · Coffee · Modern Pastry
Devas' Lounge

The softest corner of the resort. Morning coffee, afternoon pastry, and a lounge sensibility that earns its place between the intensity of the fine dining floors and the energy of the beach. Modern patisserie with tropical influence — local fruit, fermented components, precise sugar work.

Botanical Cocktail Bar
Sipping Tiger

The bar as a culinary space. Botanical cocktails built from the same local ingredient network that feeds the kitchen — fermented shrubs, tropical infusions, house-made bitters, and spirits chosen for provenance as much as flavour. Where the culinary philosophy goes liquid.

Villa & Room Service
In-Room Dining

Pinto extended — the fire kitchen's menu brought to the villa or room. The same sourcing, the same culinary standards, without the dining room. In-room dining at this level is not a convenience; it is a service that has to meet the expectations set by the restaurants on property.

9
Outlets
90%
Local ingredients

Sustainability programs
The Pipeline

Concepts in development

The studio is always building. Three concepts currently in active development — each one a different expression of what Cultivate Studio believes a restaurant can be. Some are almost ready. Some are still taking shape. All of them are worth watching.

Concept 01
Venetian Casual Fine Dining · Phuket
In Development
M E U S
Crafted Venetian Heritage · Phuket

Where Venice meets
the Andaman.

Meus celebrates the culinary heritage of the Venetian Laguna and the Veneto region. Every dish rooted in tradition, crafted from seasonal and sustainable ingredients, served in a space that makes fine dining feel like coming home.

The aperitivo ritual is at the heart of the experience — where Venetians gather in the piazzas before dinner, at Meus that spirit comes to Phuket. Cocktails, small bites, conversation. The stage is set before the first course arrives.

Vision

Phuket's most celebrated Italian dining destination

Mission

Veneto heritage through authentic flavors and aperitivo culture

What sets it apart

Fine dining made accessible through craft, ritual, and seasonal sourcing

Concept 02
Thai Fine Dining · Phuket
In Development
J A R A S
The New Space · Phuket

Same soul.
New architecture.

Jaras — Michelin-listed, Thailand's Favourite, WWF partner — is evolving. The culinary identity that earned its recognition stays intact. What changes is the space it inhabits and the scale of the vision.

A new dining room designed to match the depth of the food it serves. Grand in scale, considered in every detail. Arched ceilings, green marble, copper — all of it in service of a cuisine rooted in Southern Thailand and the sea.

★ Michelin Thailand's Favourite WWF Partner Sustainability Award
Concept 03
Concept Bar · Phuket
Taking Shape
N 4 7
07°53'N 98°24'E · Phuket

Named after a
nautical coordinate.

N47 takes its name from the nautical coordinate of Phuket — 07°53'N 98°24'E. A bar that connects the island's maritime history to the culture of Venice, where water, trade, and hospitality have always met.

Three historical threads woven into one space: the maritime trade routes that connected Venice and the Andaman Sea, the water cultures of two cities built on lagoons, and the cultural crossroads where East meets West over a glass.

N47, Meus, and the new Jaras — three concepts in one destination.

A quarterly research letter

The Long Table

Written for the people who build hospitality — not the people who review it. Each issue covers one subject in depth: an ingredient, a technique, a restaurant worth watching, a system worth building. Slow, considered, and genuinely useful.

Published slowly. Written carefully. Shared freely.

◈
The Ingredient

One deep dive per issue — provenance, technique, producers, and where it's being used well right now. Not a trend report. A field note.

⬡
The Movement

One restaurant or concept worth watching before the guides find it. The underground pioneers driving the next shift in hospitality.

✦
The System

One operational or sustainability insight from active kitchen and consulting work — the infrastructure behind great restaurants.

Subscribe to receive each issue directly. No noise, no frequency pressure. Just good thinking, four times a year.
